summary: 2026-06-06 22:27:08 PDT. Verdict: the brief's hypothesis is established from the bundled SDK types and repo wiring. (1) `connect`/`postMessage`/`onMessage` is not valid for `inMeeting` -> `inCamera`; it is meeting <-> main-client/mirrored-app communication. The `inCamera` `connect()` rejection is fundamental, not a retry timing issue. (2) The intended Layers path is to spawn/draw the camera rendering instance with Layers APIs; `onRenderedAppOpened` is only a readiness/diagnostic event, not a payload channel. For live panel-computed data, use an out-of-band channel such as the existing Node backend; no bundled type exposes a panel -> camera message API. I cannot inspect the official sample without network or bundled source, but the SDK shape implies dynamic camera data must be fetched/derived by the rendering webview or supplied by app backend. (3) A server relay is the right call under the constraints: panel POSTs latest aggregate state, camera SSEs or short-polls the latest snapshot, state is ephemeral and meeting/presenter-scoped. (4) I do not recommend a `postMessage` minimal fix; if a live test somehow proves undocumented `inCamera` delivery, the fix would be to stop requiring camera `connect()`, gate the first send on `onRenderedAppOpened`, and instrument receive, but that contradicts the declarations. (5) Main risks are leaking/corrupting relay state: use aggregate-only validation, authenticated writes, non-PII keys/logs, TTL cleanup, replay-latest behavior for camera reloads, and latency handling around the 1s tick.

findings.title: SDK messaging cannot feed inCamera
findings.claim: `RealZoom.init()` registers `onMessage`/`onConnect` and calls `connect()` for both panel and camera, then `postMessage()` queues/sends state as a side-panel -> camera bridge at lines 377-395. The SDK declarations contradict that model: `connect()` can only be called in meeting and connects to the app in the main client (`sdk.d.ts:4485`), `onConnect` is only received in meeting for that main-client connection (`sdk.d.ts:4986-4989`), `postMessage` targets the mirrored app (`sdk.d.ts:4504`), and `onMessage` supported running contexts exclude `inCamera` (`sdk.d.ts:5006`). Since `OverlayApp` only updates from `adapter.onMessage()` (`client/src/components/OverlayApp.jsx:15-17`), the camera instance has no supported receive path and the overlay freezes.
findings.suggestion: Remove `connect`/`postMessage`/`onMessage` from the camera-overlay data path and replace it with an out-of-band relay; keep app-instance messaging only for true meeting <-> main-client mirroring if the product still needs it.

findings.title: Add a backend relay for overlay snapshots
findings.claim: The existing backend only exposes health/log/OAuth/static routes (`server/src/app.js:65-87`), so there is no supported channel for the spawned `inCamera` webview to obtain the panel-computed snapshot once SDK instance messaging is ruled out. A relay can satisfy the brief's cadence and reload requirement by storing the latest aggregate state and replaying it to the camera instance.
findings.suggestion: Add relay endpoints such as panel `POST /api/overlay/:channel` and camera `GET /api/overlay/:channel/stream` via SSE, or short poll if SSE is unreliable. Store only the latest snapshot plus sequence/updatedAt, replay immediately on subscribe, expire stale channels, and delete on stop/end.

findings.title: Relay must enforce aggregate-only privacy
findings.claim: `buildOverlayState()` currently emits aggregate fields only and leaves `prefs` empty, which matches the privacy constraint. Once this crosses the server, trusting the client shape is not enough; reusing `/api/log` would be especially wrong because it logs arbitrary JSON bodies (`server/src/app.js:70-72`). Poor keying or logging could leak meeting cost data across meetings/users or expose private identifiers.
findings.suggestion: Validate an exact aggregate schema server-side, reject extra fields or non-empty private payloads, do not log snapshots, hash/redact identifiers in diagnostics, rate-limit writes, and key channels by a meeting-scoped plus presenter-scoped identifier rather than raw public meeting ID alone.

findings.title: Relay identity details need live confirmation
findings.claim: The relay design needs both panel and camera to derive the same channel key. The app currently requests `getMeetingContext` but not `getMeetingUUID` in `ZOOM_CAPABILITIES`, while the SDK exposes `getMeetingUUID()` with `meetingUUID` and optional `parentUUID` (`sdk.d.ts:1204-1208`). It also needs confirmation that `inCamera` can call the chosen identity APIs and maintain fetch/SSE or polling reliably under Zoom.
findings.suggestion: In the next live run, add `getMeetingUUID` if used, log redacted `runningContext`, `meetingID`/`meetingUUID`/`parentUUID`, and `participantUUID` from both instances, then test camera short-poll and SSE. Use the result to choose the channel key and auth scheme.

findings.title: Comments encode the wrong bridge model
findings.claim: The adapter comments describe `connect`/`postMessage`/`onMessage` as a side-panel <-> camera context state bridge, and the mock loopback makes that impossible channel look valid in local tests. This will mislead future work after the relay is introduced.
findings.suggestion: Update comments and mocks to describe the relay/latest-snapshot behavior; keep SDK instance-communication tests separate and scoped to mirrored main-client behavior only.
